31.reason: [The insertion logic does not handle equal values correctly. According to the problem, right child should be used for values >= root, but this code skips insertion when data == root->data, causing missing nodes.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The insertion logic does not handle equal values correctly. According to the problem, right child should be used for values >= root, but this code skips insertion when data == root->data, causing missing nodes.]

28.reason: [The problem requires right child to be >= root, but this code ignores equal values (does not insert duplicates), causing missing nodes and incorrect leaf output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The problem requires right child to be >= root, but this code ignores equal values (does not insert duplicates), causing missing nodes and incorrect leaf output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The problem requires that right child should be inserted when data >= root->data, but this code only inserts when data > root->data, thus ignoring duplicates and violating BST insertion rule specified in the problem.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The problem requires right child to be >= root, but this code ignores equal values (does not insert duplicates), causing missing nodes and incorrect leaf output.]

41.reason: [Traversal is pre-order (visit root before children), but the problem expects left-to-right leaf order which matches in-order traversal for BST. However, since leaves are printed only when both children are NULL, and we traverse left then right, this actually produces correct left-to-right leaf order. So this is NOT an error. The real issue is in insertion of duplicates.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Traversal is pre-order (visit root before children), but the problem expects left-to-right leaf order which matches in-order traversal for BST. However, since leaves are printed only when both children are NULL, and we traverse left then right, this actually produces correct left-to-right leaf order. So this is NOT an error. The real issue is in insertion of duplicates.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The traversal order is pre-order (root, left, right), but the problem requires outputting leaf nodes from left to right, which corresponds to in-order traversal. This will output leaves in incorrect order.]
